steps.title: Identify the defining characteristic
steps.content: The passage emphasizes the delta's defining feature through two phrases: “constantly evolving” and “change in size and shape as the river deposits new sedimentary particles.” These establish that the landscape undergoes continuous physical transformation.

steps.title: Match the capacity for change
steps.content: ”Mutable” means capable of or subject to change, directly fitting the described constant transformation. The other three choices either describe unrelated properties (habitable addresses suitability for living; secluded suggests isolation) or contradict the transformation (homogeneous implies uniformity). That matches choice A — A is correct.
